title: Replication, Multi-Region Access, and Disaster Recovery · Guided Contract Studio
summary: A TypeScript pairing room incrementally builds multi-region recovery dossier, validates one changed runtime input, and keeps earlier HTTP, accessibility, security, stream, AbortSignal, and resource-budget rules active. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of multi-region recovery dossier while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: In a strict Node 24 and TypeScript 7 service, separate object durability, request availability, regional reachability, accidental deletion, corruption, account compromise, and application recovery.
In a strict Node 24 and TypeScript 7 service, configure versioning, destination ownership, IAM, KMS, filters, delete-marker behavior, replication status, metrics, and failure remediation.
In a strict Node 24 and TypeScript 7 service, evaluate Multi-Region Access Points, latency routing, active-active writes, consistency, replication lag, failover controls, and client endpoint support.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: S3, CloudFront, and TypeScript Commit to the mission model · Workshop Durability Availability Recov…
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop TypeScript file-delivery team handles case 8976 during a private media entitlement change. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to separate replication, isolated backup, restore, and failover state evidence; do not treat TypeScript types, a resolved promise, CORS, ETag, or an emulator as stronger proof than it is; then double concurrent Upload instances and preserve aggregate memory and worker budgets. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. Node and AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, PostgreSQL, credentials, host state, handle and stream checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: In a strict Node 24 and TypeScript 7 service, separate object durability, request availability, regional reachability, accidental deletion, corruption, account compromise, and application recovery.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: S3, CloudFront, and TypeScript Commit to the mission model · Workshop Durability Availability Recov….
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for in a strict node 24 and typescript 7 service, separate object durability, request availability, regional reachability, accidental deletion, corruption, account compromise, and application recovery. For S3, CloudFront, and TypeScript Commit to the mission model · Workshop Durability Availability Recov…, keep Durability Availability Recovery connected to executable TypeScript storage and delivery decision evidence.

steps.title: S3, CloudFront, and TypeScript Extend the cumulative artifact · Workshop Disaster Recovery Drill
steps.instruction:
Append a compile-ready pure-TypeScript function headed "// Evidence: storage-ts-disaster-recovery-drill-1w2dex" that uses case 9186 to separate replication, isolated backup, restore, and failover state evidence. Guided rehearsal: predict the branch, make one bounded increment, and inspect peer-readable changed output. Return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not import Node or AWS SDK packages, open sockets, contact S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, PostgreSQL, networks, or external effects, read host state, execute host commands, or use credentials; verify those boundaries later with the TypeScript 7.0.2 native compiler and the @typescript/typescript6 package 6.0.2 compiler that reports 6.0.3, Node 24.18.0, AWS SDK v3.1087.0, controlled AWS, stream and handle, load, fault, security, cost, restore, and production gates.
